essence: Confused by a session mixing AngelEye and SupportSignal work, David asks Claude to run a visible background verification — start the web server and check it via Playwright MCP — instead of manual testing, before pasting results back to John.

actions.text: Run a visible background web-server + Playwright MCP check of the SupportSignal work instead of manual testing

essence: Phone update to friend Daniel: two Mac Mini M4s + 27-inch monitors + keyboards bought (~$2.5k USD, Voz approved the extra $500), Jan's machine provisioned remotely today, Mary's next — building toward a 5-machine cross-country agentic OS network with AI-to-AI file-drop communication, ahead of David's midnight tech-summit talk; team to pivot from graphics to agentic-OS videos.

actions.text: Set up Mary's Mac Mini this week to complete the 5-machine network
Deliver midnight tech-summit talk on the agentic operating system (unprepared as of call)

essence: David and Joy coordinate a Friday review meeting with the two girls (shop staff) framed as hearing what they like and don't like; David to call back in ten minutes.

While at a service counter, David iterates on concise DALL-E prompts for the smoothie menu — one fruit (mango) with three blend variations in a menu layout, no extra context — then the same pattern for the other eleven fruits.

actions.text: Generate the mango-with-three-blends menu image and prompts for the other eleven fruit concepts

essence: Brainstormed a 12-base-drink smoothie menu (banana/pineapple/papaya/coconut/strawberry/kiwi/watermelon combos with ginger, beetroot, turmeric, honey add-ons), each drink shown at the top of a menu with three small blend squares below — 12 menu designs from one framework.

David and Jan install SyncThing via Ansible (policy set: team tools go through Ansible, never manual installs) and name a root-level 'relay' folder for peer-to-peer file sync between David/Jan/Mary over Tailscale — the horizontal file channel for AI-to-AI communication in the agentic OS; live sync test deferred to tomorrow morning, with the deletion-propagation risk noted and FliHub as the eventual consumer.

actions.text: Jan to research SyncThing configuration overnight and reconvene early tomorrow to finish the relay sync test
Update agentic OS design docs with the horizontal file communication capability
Get Tailscale, remote login and SSH working on Mary's machine using the session transcript

essence: Full remote provisioning of Jan's Mac: Homebrew bootstrap solves the git chicken-and-egg, Tailscale joined via auth key, SSH enabled with David's GitHub public keys, then the Ansible playbook installs the standard toolchain (FFmpeg, jq, rsync, Node/Ruby/Python, Supabase CLI, Claude Code) so versions are standardized fleet-wide; decided to split the Ansible inventory into public/private sibling repos (Jan/Mary/Lars host data private) and to do Mary's setup with Jan at his house on Saturday; Angela's sync pushed to Wednesday.

actions.text: Split Ansible into public repo + private sibling inventory, moving Tailscale IPs and Jan/Mary/Lars host data private
Document the Homebrew curl bootstrap command in the Ansible repository README
Set up Mary's machine with Jan at his house on Saturday, with Jan recording a video of the process
Push Angela's change-request sync to Wednesday

essence: Personal logistics — 1:30 meeting then Old City/Ping River/Makro trip with pickup, buy 48 sodas — plus a pre-flight nudge to Jan to have his computer running and review Tailscale install instructions before the setup session.

David directs that getting the main application up and running takes precedence over FliDeck/DACE collections-compliance work, sends edits to the mostly-correct non-functional requirements, notes the system is a good record but not yet in real production, and asks Claude to check prior session state before advising next steps — visa border run pauses him for 1-2 weeks.

actions.text: Apply David's non-functional requirement changes and check what the previous session already did before proposing next steps

essence: Nearing the end of a ~40-item functional requirements review, David logs his concerns, orders a rewrite of FR14 plus a few other changes, and notes the cross-system information is visible only to him — John wouldn't know about it.

actions.text: Rewrite FR14 and apply the remaining functional-requirement changes before moving to the next section

essence: An unintended downgrade exposed a broken upgrade script/API stack app — David orders the change undone with an absolute-path handover so AppyStack can be fixed (declaring AppyStack the canonical source of truth, .screen files never included, start.sh out of date) — then moves into SaaS B2B requirements: tier pricing marked TBA, role-based auth needed, on an aggressive solo-dev-plus-AI timeline with data conflicts flagged as the bigger risk.

actions.text: Undo the unintended downgrade and produce a handover with correct absolute paths + instructions so AppyStack gets fixed
Add SaaS B2B tier (pricing TBA) and role-based authentication requirements into the system
Report the out-of-date start.sh startup-script malfunction back with details

essence: David asks how to kick off the BMAD method — what the NP command is and what steps he should expect through the process.
